item.title: Image provenance
item.content:encoded:
This is an artwork called (sigh) "Untitled", by artistic photographer Wai Ming Ng.

https://cargocollective.com/waimingng/photography-Untitled

I see no indication that any of Wai Ming Ng's works are available under Creative Commons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A good example of an article that doesn't require heaps and heaps of testing logs or a tacked-on backstory or a super-spooky plot twist at the end to keep the reader interested. The implications and mystery surrounding this skip are fascinating, and I'm happy with giving it a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The scenery does loop. The last sentence of the second paragraph under Description says you can go 6 kilometers out from the center, and then find yourself on the opposite edge, facing back toward the center again.

As for the shape… that is an excellent question, up there with "is there a ceiling?", "where do the animal tracks come from?", and "why does the description specify that stuff left inside persists if the door is closed?".

I instinctively want answers to these, but at the same time, I think it makes sense that the Foundation doesn't have them. This is a very well-contained SCP that doesn't seem to pose any risk of hurting anyone or breaching containment; it's one of their lowest priorities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
UGH, IT'S NOT SPOOPY -1000
(kidding)

as a longtime lurker, I really like this. it reminds me of the old skips that were weird for the sake of weird. not for a tale, or a GOI, or subtle spoop, or for some OTHER purpose, but something simply ANOMALOUS that needs to be contained.

+1. Well executed. stellar stuff, Dex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, does the scenery loop? it's 144 km^2. Is it square, or some other shape?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but agree that there isnt enough, it just seems like there's so much that could be there that isnt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Groovy. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really, really like this. Well done, Dex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, Gears. Means a lot coming from you. And that's exactly what I was going for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. It feels like a throwback to the early days…just a "what the hell?" thing, short and sweet…very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even if the rest of the SCP weren't so neat, "Foundation Property Acquisition Techniques" would net a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Made one minor edit (repetitive phrasing), if that is okay.

I love the complete ambiguity of this one, and the last line about purpose ended the whole entry on a perfect note. There's almost something vaguely romantic, but unstable, about the whole idea. Best thing about it is the sense of atmosphere, complimented by the image. Definite upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No no, it's saying that the walls that SHOULD comprise the closet are actually still there, but this 6km radius of tundra has somehow been overlaid on top/around/through its space.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple-but-different take on spacial anomalies. I can dig this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nnno. I actually had to google what this story was. While I can see the similarities, I did not intend a reference here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, no kidding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seemed like a standard "Space inside a space", but then I realized that the Walls are Still There. That brings up a whole host of other questions about the room and its origin. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait. Is this SCP a reference to that short story, The Veldt?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait…

It can not been determined

You meant to say "has" instead of "can", right? If so, ignore my last post (didn't want to assume and edit it).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Where's the agent in the picture?

Under the tree in the foreground, on the left. Maybe his actual name is Agent Wally?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[EDIT: chasing typos, now fixed, so nevermind.]

Great overall, though, don't get me wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Helium. Balloons. Do it.

Also, this one's great. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) Yes, but what if the ceiling is two miles up? The smoke wouldn't reveal it, it'd be past clouds and too high.

2) Yes, there are seasons. I'll edit that in.

3) Yes, in theory, it could be used to test or house other SCPs. It's the base SCP which Bio-Site 66 is centered upon; i have referenced this Site with several of my older SCP articles. But as we all know, it's a terrible idea to mix SCPs we don't understand together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's mostly finished. This line is saying "we can't tell if the sky keeps going into space, or if it's unlimited".

item.title: Re: Taiga <-> Veldt
item.content:encoded: A recently deleted SCP used the phrase "in a room under the guise of a tundra" in its containment procedures. I didn't wanna spam my SCP's discussion thread, so i just edited this post instead of replying to Sophos's after I was shown the reference. It was completely unintentional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great weird science. I mean, it's just a room full of taiga; what the hell, seriously? Why is this a thing? It doesn't need more, though I was expecting there to be something else; I'm just not disappointed that it didn't happen. That I expected it meant that it would have been cliche to, say, have there be things living out there, or any of the other anomalous properties mentioned not to exist. It's enough as it is.

Where's the agent in the picture?

Also, I fixed a typo for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one just didn't grip me in any way, and I'm haze on the walls still being detectable bit. Is that the the limit of the 12 km that they're detectable? That there is not, in fact, a taiga, and people just take really, really small steps to fool themselves into thinking they're traveling that far? Taking fifteen or so steps runs you into the wall despite there seeming to more taiga left? Confused.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this as well. I wouldn't mind seeing a bit of a test log. Seems like a smoky fire would reveal a ceiling or lack thereof. Plus, wind patterns. Are there seasons? Could it be used to test/house other SCPs or maybe has an optional vacation spot for personnel? Lots of potential here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, short, sweet and to the point.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it as it is, short and simple.

item.title: Re: Taiga <-> Veldt
item.content:encoded: I still don't get it. Can anyone care to shed some light?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really wish there was more to this article. Really, really. It's like I'm waiting for a hook that doesn't come.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, the picture adds much to the entry. It is clearly incomplete, especially about the ceiling/sky. Please finish this intriguing SCP.

It can not been determined whether or not SCP-1479 has a proper ceiling

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No pictures in the forums. -Bright

Sorry, won't do that again.

+1

item.title: Re: Taiga <-> Veldt
item.content:encoded:
No problem.

Also, someone explain why people keep saying something about a "room in the guise of a tundra". It feels like when people quote HGttG, i have no idea what the reference is. Oh god.

item.title: Taiga <-> Veldt
item.content:encoded:
This SCP reminds me of the short story, “The Veldt”, my interest is piqued but it reads like an incomplete SCP.
I also feel like I'm missing something important that's contributing to my feeling that it's incomplete so I have no idea how to feel about this.
I guess I'll stay with a neutral vote for now (Sorry Dex!).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh Bastet.
So you managed to do a room "in guise of a tundra" as an actual SCP.
+1
